what_it_does: Defines an elevation and texture token layer: a warm-tinted shadow colour, a six-step elevation ramp (--elev-0..--elev-5) each built from an ambient + key shadow layer, a gold active-glow, an inset/pressed shadow, and grain/hairline texture tokens. Provides elevation helper classes (.e0..-.e5, .glow-active) and a .grain utility that paints an inline SVG fractal-noise overlay.
observations: --shadow-c is a warm hsl base mixed into each elevation via color-mix(in srgb, var(--shadow-c) N%, transparent)
Elevation ramp roles are documented: e0 flush, e1 raised card, e2 hover, e3 popover/menu, e4 modal, e5 dragged/floating; opacity and blur grow with the step
--glow-active combines a 1px --vi-edge ring with a gold (--accent-gold) drop, reserved for the one decision element
Texture tokens: --grain-opacity 0.05, --grain-scale 180px, --hairline and --hairline-strong as shadow-tinted color-mix
.grain uses isolation:isolate and an ::after pseudo with a data:URI SVG feTurbulence fractalNoise, pointer-events:none, mix-blend-mode:multiply
Header flags tint/grain colour as placeholders the visual DNA will refine; the ramp and roles are the durable part
standouts: Shadows are warm-tinted (parchment world) rather than neutral grey, and derived from one --shadow-c so palette swaps carry through
Each elevation is an ambient+key two-layer shadow so cards read as stacked sheets, not heavy boxes
Gold glow reserved for the single decision element, echoing the system's gold-for-the-key-thing discipline
conventions: Tokenised ramp instead of ad-hoc box-shadows; consumers use --elev-* / helper classes
color-mix derivation from a single base colour for palette-swap safety
@kind inline annotations on select declarations consistent with token tagging
Self-masking overlay (pointer-events:none) so decoration never blocks interaction
notes: A warm-tinted elevation ramp (e0-e5, ambient+key two-layer shadows) plus a gold active-glow, inset, and an SVG-noise grain overlay; aesthetic values are placeholders but the ramp/roles are durable.
questions: Where are --vi-edge and --accent-gold defined that this file references?
The header says tint/grain are placeholders the visual DNA will refine - has that refinement happened or is it pending?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header promises a tokenised warm elevation ramp plus subtle grain texture, and the file delivers the ramp, glow, inset, hairlines, and the grain utility.
apparent_themes: depth/texture as a tokenised dimension, not ad-hoc shadows
warmth/material coherence (parchment world, sheets not boxes)
reserved gold emphasis for the decision element
intention_signals: Aims to give the surface a felt material depth (lightly stacked warm sheets with subtle paper tooth) through a coherent elevation ramp and texture tokens, all derived from one shadow colour so the look stays consistent under palette changes.
novelty: Warm-tinted, two-layer (ambient+key) elevation expressed as a tokenised ramp tied to a parchment aesthetic, with an inline SVG fractal-noise grain overlay as a reusable utility.
contribution: Contributes the depth/elevation vocabulary: the warm shadow base, the e0-e5 ramp with named roles, the gold active-glow, inset, hairline tokens, and the self-masking grain texture utility.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS file defining a warm-tinted elevation ramp (--elev-0..5, ambient+key shadows), a gold active-glow, inset and hairline tokens, and a grain texture utility using an inline SVG fractal-noise overlay, with helper elevation classes.
intention_for_embedding: To add a coherent, palette-resilient depth and texture dimension (stacked warm sheets, subtle paper grain) as tokenised elevation roles rather than ad-hoc box-shadows, reserving a gold glow for the decision element.
